queries.query: Cursor Bugbot faster cheaper more bugs June 2026
Cursor semantic search higher accuracy codebase questions
Cursor automations review monitoring cloud agents
OpenAI Codex CLI install terminal coding agent
Anthropic Claude Code quality postmortem evals gradual rollouts
Anthropic demystifying evals agents regression suite
Anthropic contain Claude sandbox egress controls
Vercel AI Gateway production reliability failover
OpenAI GPT-5 mini SWE-bench pricing developers
Anthropic prompt caching codebase assistant latency cost

selected_sources.title: Run Bugbot before you push
selected_sources.why_selected: Cursor says Bugbot is over 3x faster, 22% cheaper, finds 10% more bugs per review, and 90% of runs now finish in under 3 minutes.

selected_sources.title: Index the repo so agents can search by meaning, not just strings
selected_sources.why_selected: Cursor reports 12.5% higher codebase QA accuracy on average, 0.3% higher code retention overall, 2.6% higher retention on repos with 1,000+ files, and 2.2% more dissatisfied follow-ups when semantic search is unavailable.

selected_sources.title: Use always-on cloud agents for review, triage, and incident chores
selected_sources.why_selected: Cursor says Bugbot-like automations run thousands of times a day and have caught millions of bugs; its internal security-review automation has caught multiple vulnerabilities and critical bugs; Runlayer says the setup helps them move faster than teams five times their size.

selected_sources.title: Add Codex CLI as a terminal-first coding agent
selected_sources.why_selected: OpenAI says GPT-5-Codex can work for more than 7 hours on complex tasks, cached cloud tasks cut median completion time by 90%, bottom-decile turns use 93.7% fewer tokens than GPT-5, and Codex reviews the vast majority of internal PRs while catching hundreds of issues per day.

selected_sources.title: Treat system-prompt edits like code changes
selected_sources.why_selected: Anthropic reports that one verbosity instruction caused a 3% drop on both Opus 4.6 and 4.7 in a broader evaluation set, and it immediately reverted the change.

selected_sources.title: Graduate capability tests into an always-on regression suite
selected_sources.why_selected: Anthropic recommends capability tasks that later graduate into a continuous regression suite and notes that fixing eval bugs plus scaffold issues moved one CORE-Bench result from 42% to 95%.

selected_sources.title: Design containment at the environment layer first
selected_sources.why_selected: Anthropic says its Claude Code sandbox cut permission prompts by 84%, auto mode catches about 83% of overeager behaviors, and Gray Swan prompt-injection success is roughly 0.1% on single attempts.

selected_sources.title: Put a gateway in front of providers so outages do not stop the app
selected_sources.why_selected: Vercel says AI Gateway is the same system that has powered v0 for millions of users, sits on infrastructure delivering sub-20ms latency, and removes the single-provider failure mode.

selected_sources.title: Make GPT-5 mini the default coding workhorse
selected_sources.why_selected: OpenAI reports GPT-5 mini scores 71.0% on SWE-bench Verified and 71.6% on Aider polyglot while costing $0.25 per 1M input tokens and $2 per 1M output tokens.

selected_sources.title: Cache stable repo context instead of resending it every turn
selected_sources.why_selected: Anthropic says prompt caching cuts cost by up to 90% and latency by up to 85%; one 100k-token prompt dropped from 11.5s to 2.4s time-to-first-token.

discarded_candidates.title: Official GitHub MCP server
discarded_candidates.reason: Strong setup docs but not enough measured speed or robustness evidence for promoted jam.

discarded_candidates.title: How Anthropic teams use Claude Code
discarded_candidates.reason: Useful anecdotes, but the stronger jam came from the operational docs and postmortem-style sources.

discarded_candidates.title: Full GPT-5 as the default for every task
discarded_candidates.reason: The better weekly recommendation is cheap-by-default routing with explicit escalation rules.
